speaker: POLONIUS
text: He will come straight. Look you lay home to him.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Tell him his pranks have been too broad to bear with

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And that your Grace hath screened and stood between

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Much heat and him. I’ll silence me even here.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Pray you, be round with him.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Mother, mother, mother!

speaker: QUEEN
text: I’ll warrant you. Fear me not. Withdraw,

speaker: QUEEN
text: I hear him coming.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Now, mother, what’s the matter?

speaker: QUEEN
text: Hamlet, thou hast thy father much offended.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Mother, you have my father much offended.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Come, come, you answer with an idle tongue.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Go, go, you question with a wicked tongue.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Why, how now, Hamlet?

speaker: HAMLET
text: What’s the matter now?

speaker: QUEEN
text: Have you forgot me?

speaker: HAMLET
text: No, by the rood, not so.

speaker: HAMLET
text: You are the Queen, your husband’s brother’s wife,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And (would it were not so) you are my mother.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Nay, then I’ll set those to you that can speak.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Come, come, and sit you down; you shall not budge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: You go not till I set you up a glass

speaker: HAMLET
text: Where you may see the inmost part of you.

speaker: QUEEN
text: What wilt thou do? Thou wilt not murder me?

speaker: QUEEN
text: Help, ho!

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What ho! Help!

speaker: HAMLET
text: How now, a rat? Dead for a ducat, dead.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: O, I am slain!

speaker: QUEEN
text: O me, what hast thou done?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nay, I know not. Is it the King?

speaker: QUEEN
text: O, what a rash and bloody deed is this!

speaker: HAMLET
text: A bloody deed—almost as bad, good mother,

speaker: HAMLET
text: As kill a king and marry with his brother.

speaker: QUEEN
text: As kill a king?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ay, lady, it was my word.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Thou wretched, rash, intruding fool, farewell.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I took thee for thy better. Take thy fortune.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Thou find’st to be too busy is some danger.

speaker: HAMLET
text:
To
                 
                Queen
                .
                 
              Leave wringing of your hands. Peace, sit you down,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And let me wring your heart; for so I shall

speaker: HAMLET
text: If it be made of penetrable stuff,

speaker: HAMLET
text: If damnèd custom have not brazed it so

speaker: HAMLET
text: That it be proof and bulwark against sense.

speaker: QUEEN
text: What have I done, that thou dar’st wag thy tongue

speaker: QUEEN
text: In noise so rude against me?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Such an act

speaker: HAMLET
text: That blurs the grace and blush of modesty,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Calls virtue hypocrite, takes off the rose

speaker: HAMLET
text: From the fair forehead of an innocent love

speaker: HAMLET
text: And sets a blister there, makes marriage vows

speaker: HAMLET
text: As false as dicers’ oaths—O, such a deed

speaker: HAMLET
text: As from the body of contraction plucks

speaker: HAMLET
text: The very soul, and sweet religion makes

speaker: HAMLET
text: A rhapsody of words! Heaven’s face does glow

speaker: HAMLET
text: O’er this solidity and compound mass

speaker: HAMLET
text: With heated visage, as against the doom,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Is thought-sick at the act.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Ay me, what act

speaker: QUEEN
text: That roars so loud and thunders in the index?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Look here upon this picture and on this,

speaker: HAMLET
text: The counterfeit presentment of two brothers.

speaker: HAMLET
text: See what a grace was seated on this brow,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Hyperion’s curls, the front of Jove himself,

speaker: HAMLET
text: An eye like Mars’ to threaten and command,

speaker: HAMLET
text: A station like the herald Mercury

speaker: HAMLET
text: New-lighted on a heaven-kissing hill,

speaker: HAMLET
text: A combination and a form indeed

speaker: HAMLET
text: Where every god did seem to set his seal

speaker: HAMLET
text: To give the world assurance of a man.

speaker: HAMLET
text: This was your husband. Look you now what follows.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Here is your husband, like a mildewed ear

speaker: HAMLET
text: Blasting his wholesome brother. Have you eyes?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Could you on this fair mountain leave to feed

speaker: HAMLET
text: And batten on this moor? Ha! Have you eyes?

speaker: HAMLET
text: You cannot call it love, for at your age

speaker: HAMLET
text: The heyday in the blood is tame, it’s humble

speaker: HAMLET
text: And waits upon the judgment; and what judgment

speaker: HAMLET
text: Would step from this to this? Sense sure you have,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Else could you not have motion; but sure that sense

speaker: HAMLET
text: Is apoplexed; for madness would not err,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nor sense to ecstasy was ne’er so thralled,

speaker: HAMLET
text: But it reserved some quantity of choice

speaker: HAMLET
text: To serve in such a difference. What devil was ’t

speaker: HAMLET
text: That thus hath cozened you at hoodman-blind?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Eyes without feeling, feeling without sight,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ears without hands or eyes, smelling sans all,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Or but a sickly part of one true sense

speaker: HAMLET
text: Could not so mope. O shame, where is thy blush?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Rebellious hell,

speaker: HAMLET
text: If thou canst mutine in a matron’s bones,

speaker: HAMLET
text: To flaming youth let virtue be as wax

speaker: HAMLET
text: And melt in her own fire. Proclaim no shame

speaker: HAMLET
text: When the compulsive ardor gives the charge,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Since frost itself as actively doth burn,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And reason panders will.

speaker: QUEEN
text: O Hamlet, speak no more!

speaker: QUEEN
text: Thou turn’st my eyes into my very soul,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And there I see such black and grainèd spots

speaker: QUEEN
text: As will not leave their tinct.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nay, but to live

speaker: HAMLET
text: In the rank sweat of an enseamèd bed,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Stewed in corruption, honeying and making love

speaker: HAMLET
text: Over the nasty sty!

speaker: QUEEN
text: O, speak to me no more!

speaker: QUEEN
text: These words like daggers enter in my ears.

speaker: QUEEN
text: No more, sweet Hamlet!

speaker: HAMLET
text: A murderer and a villain,

speaker: HAMLET
text: A slave that is not twentieth part the tithe

speaker: HAMLET
text: Of your precedent lord; a vice of kings,

speaker: HAMLET
text: A cutpurse of the empire and the rule,

speaker: HAMLET
text: That from a shelf the precious diadem stole

speaker: HAMLET
text: And put it in his pocket—

speaker: QUEEN
text: No more!

speaker: HAMLET
text: A king of shreds and patches—

speaker: HAMLET
text: Save me and hover o’er me with your wings,

speaker: HAMLET
text: You heavenly guards!—What would your gracious figure?

speaker: QUEEN
text: Alas, he’s mad.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Do you not come your tardy son to chide,

speaker: HAMLET
text: That, lapsed in time and passion, lets go by

speaker: HAMLET
text: Th’ important acting of your dread command?

speaker: HAMLET
text: O, say!

speaker: GHOST
text: Do not forget. This visitation

speaker: GHOST
text: Is but to whet thy almost blunted purpose.

speaker: GHOST
text: But look, amazement on thy mother sits.

speaker: GHOST
text: O, step between her and her fighting soul.

speaker: GHOST
text: Conceit in weakest bodies strongest works.

speaker: GHOST
text: Speak to her, Hamlet.

speaker: HAMLET
text: How is it with you, lady?

speaker: QUEEN
text: Alas, how is ’t with you,

speaker: QUEEN
text: That you do bend your eye on vacancy

speaker: QUEEN
text: And with th’ incorporal air do hold discourse?

speaker: QUEEN
text: Forth at your eyes your spirits wildly peep,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And, as the sleeping soldiers in th’ alarm,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Your bedded hair, like life in excrements,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Start up and stand an end. O gentle son,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Upon the heat and flame of thy distemper

speaker: QUEEN
text: Sprinkle cool patience! Whereon do you look?

speaker: HAMLET
text: On him, on him! Look you how pale he glares.

speaker: HAMLET
text: His form and cause conjoined, preaching to stones,

speaker: HAMLET
text:
Would make them capable.
                 
                To
                 
                the
                 
                Ghost
                .
               Do not look upon me,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Lest with this piteous action you convert

speaker: HAMLET
text: My stern effects. Then what I have to do

speaker: HAMLET
text: Will want true color—tears perchance for blood.

speaker: QUEEN
text: To whom do you speak this?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Do you see nothing there?

speaker: QUEEN
text: Nothing at all; yet all that is I see.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nor did you nothing hear?

speaker: QUEEN
text: No, nothing but ourselves.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why, look you there, look how it steals away!

speaker: HAMLET
text: My father, in his habit as he lived!

speaker: HAMLET
text: Look where he goes even now out at the portal!

speaker: QUEEN
text: This is the very coinage of your brain.

speaker: QUEEN
text: This bodiless creation ecstasy

speaker: QUEEN
text: Is very cunning in.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ecstasy?

speaker: HAMLET
text: My pulse as yours doth temperately keep time

speaker: HAMLET
text: And makes as healthful music. It is not madness

speaker: HAMLET
text: That I have uttered. Bring me to the test,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And I the matter will reword, which madness

speaker: HAMLET
text: Would gambol from. Mother, for love of grace,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Lay not that flattering unction to your soul

speaker: HAMLET
text: That not your trespass but my madness speaks.

speaker: HAMLET
text: It will but skin and film the ulcerous place,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Whiles rank corruption, mining all within,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Infects unseen. Confess yourself to heaven,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Repent what’s past, avoid what is to come,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And do not spread the compost on the weeds

speaker: HAMLET
text: To make them ranker. Forgive me this my virtue,

speaker: HAMLET
text: For, in the fatness of these pursy times,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Virtue itself of vice must pardon beg,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Yea, curb and woo for leave to do him good.

speaker: QUEEN
text: O Hamlet, thou hast cleft my heart in twain!

speaker: HAMLET
text: O, throw away the worser part of it,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And live the purer with the other half!

speaker: HAMLET
text: Good night. But go not to my uncle’s bed.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Assume a virtue if you have it not.

speaker: HAMLET
text: That monster, custom, who all sense doth eat,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Of habits devil, is angel yet in this,

speaker: HAMLET
text: That to the use of actions fair and good

speaker: HAMLET
text: He likewise gives a frock or livery

speaker: HAMLET
text: That aptly is put on. Refrain tonight,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And that shall lend a kind of easiness

speaker: HAMLET
text: To the next abstinence, the next more easy;

speaker: HAMLET
text: For use almost can change the stamp of nature

speaker: HAMLET
text: And either  the devil or throw him out

speaker: HAMLET
text: With wondrous potency. Once more, good night,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And, when you are desirous to be blest,

speaker: HAMLET
text: I’ll blessing beg of you. For this same lord

speaker: HAMLET
text: I do repent; but heaven hath pleased it so

speaker: HAMLET
text: To punish me with this and this with me,

speaker: HAMLET
text: That I must be their scourge and minister.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I will bestow him and will answer well

speaker: HAMLET
text: The death I gave him. So, again, good night.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I must be cruel only to be kind.

speaker: HAMLET
text: This bad begins, and worse remains behind.

speaker: HAMLET
text: One word more, good lady.

speaker: QUEEN
text: What shall I do?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Not this by no means that I bid you do:

speaker: HAMLET
text: Let the bloat king tempt you again to bed,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Pinch wanton on your cheek, call you his mouse,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And let him, for a pair of reechy kisses

speaker: HAMLET
text: Or paddling in your neck with his damned fingers,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Make you to ravel all this matter out

speaker: HAMLET
text: That I essentially am not in madness,

speaker: HAMLET
text: But mad in craft. ’Twere good you let him know,

speaker: HAMLET
text: For who that’s but a queen, fair, sober, wise,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Would from a paddock, from a bat, a gib,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Such dear concernings hide? Who would do so?

speaker: HAMLET
text: No, in despite of sense and secrecy,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Unpeg the basket on the house’s top,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Let the birds fly, and like the famous ape,

speaker: HAMLET
text: To try conclusions, in the basket creep

speaker: HAMLET
text: And break your own neck down.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Be thou assured, if words be made of breath

speaker: QUEEN
text: And breath of life, I have no life to breathe

speaker: QUEEN
text: What thou hast said to me.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I must to England, you know that.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Alack,

speaker: QUEEN
text: I had forgot! ’Tis so concluded on.

speaker: HAMLET
text: There’s letters sealed; and my two schoolfellows,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Whom I will trust as I will adders fanged,

speaker: HAMLET
text: They bear the mandate; they must sweep my way

speaker: HAMLET
text: And marshal me to knavery. Let it work,

speaker: HAMLET
text: For ’tis the sport to have the enginer

speaker: HAMLET
text: Hoist with his own petard; and ’t shall go hard

speaker: HAMLET
text: But I will delve one yard below their mines

speaker: HAMLET
text: And blow them at the moon. O, ’tis most sweet

speaker: HAMLET
text: When in one line two crafts directly meet.

speaker: HAMLET
text: This man shall set me packing.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I’ll lug the guts into the neighbor room.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Mother, good night indeed. This counselor

speaker: HAMLET
text: Is now most still, most secret, and most grave,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Who was in life a foolish prating knave.—

speaker: HAMLET
text: Come, sir, to draw toward an end with you.—

speaker: HAMLET
text: Good night, mother.
